title: IDF Strikes Central Gaza, Kills ~10 Hamas Operatives
detail: The IDF announced on March 29, 2026 (Palm Sunday) that it struck and killed approximately 10 armed Hamas operatives in central Gaza overnight. The IDF stated the individuals were identified as planning attacks on Israeli forces. Palestinian civil defense teams recovered bodies from the strike sites. The strikes drew condemnation from Palestinian officials who characterized them as further ceasefire violations. The broader context was the ongoing Iran-Israel war, with Israeli Air Force aircraft simultaneously conducting 120+ bombing sorties against Iranian missile sites near Tehran — a major escalation that had begun February 28, 2026.

title: US Ambassador Waltz at UN: Gaza's Future Depends on Hamas Disarming
detail: US Ambassador to the UN Mike Waltz told the Security Council on March 25, 2026: 'The future of Gaza is entirely dependent now on Hamas decommissioning its weapons. We truly stand at an inflection point now.' UN Gaza High Representative Nickolay Mladenov, briefing the Security Council for the first time on March 24, 2026, outlined the disarmament framework's four principles: sequencing (most dangerous weapons first), verification (reconstruction tied to decommissioning), people-centered reintegration, and guarantor oversight. Mladenov urged Council members to press Hamas to accept 'without delay.' Commentary intensified through March 29 as Palestinian critics called the plan 'blackmailing the victim.'
